opportunities.specific_product: Best Choice Products upholstered dorm headboard (slips behind bed frame, no tools)
opportunities.transformation_hook: Turned a $39 dorm bed into a boutique-hotel nook before move-in
opportunities.before_state: Bare dorm mattress shoved against a cinderblock wall, no back support, plastic desk chair energy
opportunities.after_state: Tufted headboard nook with string lights and throw pillows, looks like a hotel headboard photo
opportunities.why_now: Apartment Therapy's Pinterest feed is running a dedicated dorm-decor cluster (headboard, curtains, diffuser, mirror, towel) right as late-July back-to-school shopping ramps up ahead of August move-in.

opportunities.specific_product: Amazon floor-to-ceiling linen-look blackout curtains, hung high-and-wide
opportunities.transformation_hook: Designer's $34 Amazon curtain hack made my rental look expensive
opportunities.before_state: Bare windows or short mini blinds, ceilings look low, room feels unfinished and cheap
opportunities.after_state: Floor-to-ceiling curtains hung near the ceiling, room reads taller, brighter, and expensive
opportunities.why_now: Real Simple's top pin this cycle is literally 'interior designer's Amazon-only luxe upgrade tips, window treatments included' — a direct buyer-intent signal for exactly this before/after.

opportunities.specific_product: Mamma Mia waterproof stretch sofa cover (pet-hair-proof)
opportunities.transformation_hook: $52 couch cover hid 3 years of pet hair and stains overnight
opportunities.before_state: Worn, shedding-covered, stained sofa nobody wants to sit on
opportunities.after_state: Tailored-looking stretch cover, sofa looks brand-new and wipes clean of pet hair
opportunities.why_now: Highest-commission brand in our priority list (24-30%) with an evergreen but consistently strong Amazon purchase pattern for home/kitchen — pet-hair pain point is always in-season and easy to dramatize as before/after.

opportunities.specific_product: LED vanity light bar + bathtub caddy tray bundle
opportunities.transformation_hook: $28 light bar swap made our guest bath look like a spa
opportunities.before_state: Dim yellow builder-grade bathroom light, dated brass fixture, guests avoid the mirror
opportunities.after_state: Bright LED vanity bar plus a new bathtub caddy tray — spa-like glow, Pinterest-ready guest bath
opportunities.why_now: HGTV's Pinterest feed is clustering three separate bathroom posts right now (guest-bath prep, bathtub trends, task/ambient/accent lighting) — a clear signal bathroom refresh content is about to surface.

opportunities.specific_product: Pull-out cabinet organizer bins / lazy-susan set
opportunities.transformation_hook: $24 pull-out organizers made our tiny kitchen feel twice the size
opportunities.before_state: Cluttered cabinets with pots and pans avalanching out, counters piled with overflow
opportunities.after_state: Neat pull-out organizers with everything visible and sorted, clear counters, kitchen 'feels bigger'
opportunities.why_now: Both BHG ('what makes a kitchen well-designed, common mistakes to avoid') and Real Simple ('designer tricks to expand a kitchen without tearing down walls') are running small-kitchen content simultaneously — cross-source confirmation of rising interest.
